open_points.question: Should duplicate route audit result records for the same expected route identity be rejected or tolerated during accounting and reconciliation?
Should route audit result records whose route identity was not present in the launch snapshot be rejected or ignored?
Does any failed, cancelled, or timed out route audit block synthesis of the final route audit report?
May the control plane emit a partial final route audit report when one or more expected route audit identities remain unresolved?

scenarios.expected: 40 expected route identities are launched, 40 terminal route audit result records are received with no duplicates or unknown identities, the batch settles, the report layer emits a complete route audit report manifest, and final reconciliation matches the observed final report records.

one or more expected route identities have no received route audit result record, unresolved identities remain after settle, and the complete label is not satisfied unless a separate partial policy allows synthesis.

a received route audit result record repeats an already represented expected route identity, duplicate detection is raised in accounting or reconciliation, and completion depends on the open duplicate policy.

one or more route audits reach terminal status failed, the batch can still settle because failed is join-eligible, and whether final report synthesis is blocked remains governed by the open failure policy.

one or more route audits reach terminal status timed_out, the batch can still settle because timed_out is join-eligible, and whether final report synthesis is blocked remains governed by the open failure policy.

a received route audit result record represents a route identity absent from the launch snapshot, it does not satisfy join eligibility for any expected identity, and handling remains governed by the open unknown policy.

if unresolved, failed, cancelled, or timed out route audits are treated as disqualifying for synthesis, the control plane refuses to mark the final route audit report complete and records the batch as incomplete.

if policy allows incomplete synthesis, the control plane may emit a partial final route audit report with unresolved or non-success terminal statuses accounted for and without applying the complete label.

If policy is later set to reject partial reporting, unresolved or excluded checks prevent complete reporting and no partial security report is accepted.
If policy is later set to allow incomplete reporting, a partial security report may be emitted with unresolved or excluded checks explicitly accounted for as incomplete.

If one or more expected routes report terminal status failed while all 60 expected identities are still represented exactly once, await may wake because failed is eligible, accounting records the failed status, and whether synthesis is blocked remains open under failure_policy.

If one or more expected routes report terminal status timed_out while all 60 expected identities are still represented exactly once, await may wake because timed_out is eligible, accounting records the timed_out status, and whether synthesis is blocked remains open under failure_policy.

If a received route result record represents an identity outside route-001 through route-060, that identity must not satisfy join eligibility, accounting must flag an unknown represented identity from observed records, and batch completeness cannot be labeled complete while unknown_policy is unresolved.

Using the observed set where route-007 is missing and route-008 is duplicated, if partial_policy is later decided as refuse partial output, the control plane should stop before emitting a complete or incomplete batch summary and record the batch as not synthesizable.

Using the observed set where route-007 is missing and route-008 is duplicated, if partial_policy is later decided as allow_incomplete, the control plane may emit an explicitly incomplete batch summary that preserves duplicate and unresolved accounting findings without labeling the batch complete.

If received_route_result_records exceed the control-plane fan_in_summary_input_budget of 60 route result records per batch summary synthesis, the control plane must treat the over-budget input as a control_plane_input capacity condition and avoid silently claiming complete synthesis.

When expected identities are unresolved, such as missing route-007 with a duplicate route-008, should the control plane refuse summary output or allow an explicitly incomplete summary?

scenarios.expected: When exactly one terminal result record is received for each of route-001 through route-060 with no duplicates and no unknown identities, await wakes on audit_route_result_batch_settled, the batch can satisfy all complete_label_requires, and reconciliation can mark the summary complete.

Given expected identities route-001 through route-060, if 60 records are received but route-007 is absent, accounting reports route-007 in expected_minus_observed_route_identities and completeness is not satisfied regardless of total record count.

Given expected identities route-001 through route-060, if 60 records are received and route-008 appears twice while route-007 is absent, represented identities include a duplicate for route-008, no_duplicates fails, and the batch cannot receive a complete label.

If one or more expected routes report terminal status failed while all expected identities are otherwise represented exactly once, await still wakes because failed is eligible, and downstream synthesis behavior remains open under failure_policy.

If one or more expected routes report terminal status timed_out while all expected identities are otherwise represented exactly once, await still wakes because timed_out is eligible, and completeness depends on identity and duplicate checks rather than success-only outcomes.

If a received terminal result record carries an identity outside route-001 through route-060, that identity is excluded from join eligibility, accounting flags no_unknown_identities as unsatisfied, and policy handling remains open under unknown_policy.

For the observed set with route-007 missing and route-008 duplicated, the control plane may refuse to emit downstream summary output and instead record an unresolved incomplete batch if partial_policy is resolved to reject.

For the observed set with route-007 missing and route-008 duplicated, the control plane may emit an explicitly incomplete summary manifest while preserving duplicate and unresolved-identity findings if partial_policy is resolved to allow_incomplete.

open_points.question: Do failed or timed_out expected child outcomes block synthesis or only block a complete label?
May the control plane emit an incomplete batch summary when quorum is reached but reconciliation still shows unresolved or unknown identities?
Should unknown child result identities be rejected outright or preserved in an incomplete report?
If multiple records arrive for the same expected child identity, should later reporting reject them or tolerate them as duplicates?

scenarios.expected: After six succeeded results for six distinct expected child identities are observed, quorum k=6 on successful_outcomes is satisfied and audit_batch_settled wakes the control plane. Later accounting sees 10 expected identities, 9 represented expected identities in received_route_result_records, 1 unknown identity record, and therefore no complete label is available unless open policies allow an incomplete report.

Because one received result carries an unknown identity, one expected child identity remains in expected_minus_observed_route_identities. That missing expected identity cannot satisfy join readiness, does not prevent the already-met quorum wake, and prevents complete_label_requires from being fully satisfied.

If any received_route_result_records repeat an already represented expected child identity, quorum counting must be by distinct eligible identity so duplicates do not create extra quorum credit. Reporting disposition for the duplicate record remains open under duplicate_policy.

Two expected children reach failed terminal status after or alongside quorum satisfaction. They are counted in accounting terminal_statuses, do not contribute to successful_outcomes quorum, and whether their presence blocks synthesis or only complete labeling remains open under failure_policy.

One expected child reaches timed_out terminal status. It is accounted as terminal, contributes no successful_outcomes quorum credit, and whether its timeout blocks report emission or only complete labeling remains open under failure_policy.

The result record with an identity absent from route_launch_snapshot is ineligible for quorum, cannot satisfy the missing expected child, is surfaced by reconciliation as unknown, and leaves unknown-policy handling open.

If partial_policy is later set to reject incomplete output, the control plane may still wake on quorum after six successes but must refuse batch summary completion or publication because reconciliation shows at least one unresolved expected identity and one unknown identity, with failures/timeouts also present.

If partial_policy is later set to allow_incomplete, the control plane may emit an incomplete batch summary after quorum wake that reports 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, 1 unknown identity record, and 1 unresolved expected identity, while withholding the complete label.

open_points.question: Do failed child results block batch summary synthesis or only mark the report incomplete?
May the control plane emit or accept a partial batch report once quorum wake occurs but completeness checks still fail?
Should records carrying identities outside route_launch_snapshot be rejected or retained as diagnostic-only observations?
Should duplicate child result records be rejected or tolerated with incomplete labeling?

scenarios.expected: After 10 child identities are launched, 6 succeeded results for known identities satisfy the k=6 successful-outcomes quorum and emit audit_quorum_reached; accounting still requires completeness checks before any complete label is allowed.

If one or more launched identities have no observed result records, those identities remain in expected_minus_observed_route_identities; quorum may already have woken the control plane, but complete reporting is withheld because no_unresolved_identities fails.

If a known child identity is represented by more than one observed result record, the duplicate is detected during accounting/reconciliation; whether reporting rejects or tolerates the duplicate remains open.

Two failed known-identity results do not contribute to the successful-outcomes quorum; after quorum wake they are still counted in accounting, and whether their presence blocks synthesis remains open.
A timed-out known-identity result does not contribute to the successful-outcomes quorum; it must still be represented in accounting as a terminal status, and whether it blocks reporting remains open.

A result record for an identity not present in route_launch_snapshot never satisfies quorum eligibility and is flagged as unknown during accounting/reconciliation; acceptance versus rejection remains open.

If policy is later set to reject partial reports, the control plane may wake on quorum but must refuse any non-complete batch report when failures, unknown identity presence, duplicates, or unresolved identities remain.

If policy is later set to allow incomplete reporting, the control plane may emit or accept a partial batch summary after quorum wake while preserving incomplete labeling because complete_label_requires are not met.

batch reduction starts after the control plane wakes on quorum readiness and uses the expected child identity set plus received child result records
summary reconciliation needs the emitted batch summary manifest from the reduction layer

capacity_constraints.limit: 10 child result records plus resulting batch summary records per batch evaluation within the configured control-plane input budget

open_points.question: Should duplicate child result records be rejected or tolerated during batch accounting and summary reconciliation?
Should result records with identities not present in the route launch snapshot be rejected, ignored for reporting, or retained as incomplete evidence?
Do failed or timed out expected children block synthesis or only mark the batch summary incomplete?
May the control plane emit a partial batch report when quorum is met but complete accounting requirements are not satisfied?

scenarios.expected: When six launched child identities report succeeded, the quorum join is satisfied at k=6 and the control plane wakes on audit_quorum_reached.

If one launched child identity never appears in received_route_result_records because an unknown identity record arrived instead, batch accounting shows an unresolved expected identity and the complete label is withheld.
If any launched child identity is represented by more than one received result record, accounting flags duplicates and final completeness depends on the still-open duplicate policy.

Two expected child identities may be recorded with failed terminal status without preventing quorum once six expected identities have succeeded, but whether those failures block synthesis or only mark reporting incomplete remains open.
A timed_out terminal status for an expected child is accounted for in batch status counts and does not satisfy the success quorum, while its effect on report emission remains open.

A result record with an identity outside route_launch_snapshot cannot satisfy the quorum, is flagged as unknown during accounting, and leaves one expected identity unresolved unless that expected identity is later observed.

If policy is later set to reject partial reporting, the batch may wake on quorum yet no complete report is emitted because unknown or unresolved identities and non-success outcomes violate complete_label_requires.

If policy is later set to allow_incomplete, the batch may wake on quorum and emit a partial or explicitly incomplete report summarizing six succeeded, two failed, one timed_out, and one unknown-identity record.

If child-result or summary-record fan-in exceeds the control-plane input budget, control-plane evaluation is deferred or segmented under control-plane ownership rather than changing target data-plane execution behavior.

edges.reason: the await stage needs the launched child identity snapshot to determine quorum eligibility
accounting consumes the awakened batch context and observed child results
summary reduction depends on reconciled child-result observations and expected identities
final reconciliation compares emitted summary manifests with received summary records

open_points.question: Do failed or timed_out expected children block batch summary synthesis after quorum wake?
May the workflow emit a partial or incomplete batch summary when some expected identities remain unresolved or unknown identities are present?
Should an observed result with an identity outside route_launch_snapshot be rejected outright or tolerated as incomplete reporting?
If multiple result records appear for the same expected identity, should reporting reject them or allow incomplete reconciliation?

scenarios.expected: After 10 children are launched, the first 6 succeeded results for identities in route_launch_snapshot satisfy quorum k=6 and wake the control plane via route_batch_quorum_reached; later accounting can still withhold a complete label if reconciliation checks fail.

In the described batch, the unknown-identity result does not represent any expected child, so one launched identity remains unresolved; accounting records expected_vs_represented mismatch and unresolved identity, preventing a complete report label.

If one expected child emits duplicate result records, quorum counts only unique eligible expected identities; accounting flags duplicates and the complete report label is not available unless duplicate policy later permits special handling.

The 2 failed expected children do not reduce the already-met quorum of 6 successes, so control-plane wake still occurs; whether their failures block summary emission remains open under failure_policy.

The timed_out expected child is terminal for accounting but is not quorum-eligible for a successful_outcomes join; reporting records the timeout, and whether it blocks synthesis remains open under failure_policy.

The result carrying an identity outside route_launch_snapshot never satisfies quorum and is recorded as unknown during accounting; reconciliation also leaves one expected identity unresolved, so complete labeling fails.

If partial_policy is later decided as reject, the batch may wake on quorum but summary emission is refused because the observed set includes failures, a timeout, an unknown identity, and an unresolved expected identity.

If partial_policy is later decided as allow_incomplete, the batch may wake on quorum and emit a partial summary that explicitly remains incomplete because complete_label_requires are not all satisfied.

edges.reason: quorum eligibility is limited to identities in the launch snapshot
summary accounting compares observed child results against the expected launched identities
final reconciliation checks received batch summaries against the emitted summary manifest

open_points.question: If multiple result records arrive for one expected child identity, should reporting reject them or allow incomplete handling?
Should a child result record with an identity not present in the launch snapshot be rejected outright or retained as an incomplete-report condition?
Do failed or timed out expected children block synthesis of a batch report after quorum wake?
May the workflow emit or accept a partial batch report when quorum is met but expected identities remain unresolved or unknown identities were observed?

scenarios.expected: For a 10-child batch, once 6 distinct launched identities report status succeeded, audit_batch_quorum_reached wakes the control plane. If all 10 launched identities are later represented exactly once with terminal statuses and no unknown identities, the batch summary can be labeled complete and final reconciliation can pass.

With 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, and 1 result record carrying an identity not in the launch snapshot, quorum is still reached from the 6 known successes, but one expected launched identity remains unresolved. Reporting therefore cannot satisfy expected_vs_represented or no_unresolved_identities for a complete label.

If any of the 6 successful records is duplicated, the duplicate does not create an additional eligible identity for quorum. Complete reporting outcome remains undecided because duplicate_policy is open.

The 2 failed children are terminally accounted for and do not prevent the quorum wake once 6 known successes exist. Whether their failures block batch synthesis or only mark the report incomplete is open under failure_policy.

The timed_out child is terminally accounted for and does not satisfy a successful-outcomes quorum. Whether its timeout blocks synthesis or is allowed in a reported batch remains open under failure_policy.

The result carrying an identity outside route_launch_snapshot is ineligible for quorum and cannot satisfy the missing expected child. Its presence causes unknown and unresolved accounting conditions, so a complete label cannot be assigned.

Using the observed set of 6 succeeded, 2 failed, 1 timed_out, and 1 unknown identity, the control plane wakes at quorum but refuses to emit or accept a batch summary as reportable because partial_policy rejects incomplete accounting.

Using the same observed set, the control plane wakes at quorum and may emit or accept a batch summary only as incomplete or partial, explicitly reflecting failures, timeout, one unknown identity, and one unresolved expected identity.

edges.reason: await readiness must use the launched 50-item manifest and only counts terminal results for identities present in that launch snapshot
final synthesis needs the expected 50-item identity set from launch to compare expected versus represented results
synthesis waits for the control-plane wake event and uses the settled set of received worker result records
reconciliation compares the emitted final report summary manifest with the summary records actually received

open_points.question: What exact readiness rule should wake final synthesis for a 50-item batch when some workers never return: first terminal result, fixed timeout, quorum count, or another threshold?
Should duplicate worker result records for the same launched item be rejected, collapsed deterministically, or retained as an inconsistency that blocks complete labeling?
Should result records for identities outside the 50 launched items be rejected outright or ignored while still allowing a best-effort report?
Which failed, cancelled, or timed-out items may be ignored for synthesis, and which should block or downgrade the report?
How should a report built from fewer than all 50 launched items be labeled so consumers can distinguish complete versus partial synthesis?
How many missing or unresolved items out of 50 are acceptable before partial synthesis must be refused?

scenarios.expected: All 50 launched item identities are represented exactly once with terminal statuses, batch summary emits a complete report manifest, and final reconciliation passes all checks.
One or more launched item identities have no observed worker result records; accounting marks unresolved identities and any emitted report is treated as partial rather than complete.
Two or more observed worker result records represent the same launched item identity; accounting records duplicates and complete labeling is withheld unless a future duplicate policy resolves them.
Some launched items return terminal failed statuses; synthesis eligibility remains subject to the open failure policy, while accounting still records those failures in the batch summary.
Some launched items reach terminal timed_out without returning usable results; synthesis may still proceed on a best-effort basis, but unresolved or timed-out items prevent complete labeling.

Observed worker result records include identities not present in the 50-item launch manifest; accounting records unknown identities and complete labeling is withheld unless a future unknown-identity policy permits them.
If a future omission-threshold or readiness policy refuses the current level of incompleteness, the control plane withholds final synthesis completion and records the batch as not complete.

If a future omission-threshold and labeling policy allow the current incompleteness, the control plane emits the best possible report from represented identities and labels it as partial according to that policy.

open_points.question: What exact readiness rule should wake final synthesis for a 50-item batch when some workers never return: first terminal result, fixed timeout, quorum count, or another threshold?
Should duplicate worker result records for the same launched item be rejected, collapsed deterministically, or retained as an inconsistency that blocks complete labeling?
Should result records for identities outside the 50 launched items be rejected outright or ignored while still allowing a best-effort report?
Which failed, cancelled, or timed-out items may be ignored for synthesis, and which should block or downgrade the report?
How should a report built from fewer than all 50 launched items be labeled so consumers can distinguish complete versus partial synthesis?
How many missing or unresolved items out of 50 are acceptable before partial synthesis must be refused?

scenarios.expected: All 50 launched item identities are represented exactly once with terminal statuses, batch summary emits a complete report manifest, and final reconciliation passes all checks.
One or more launched item identities have no observed worker result records; accounting marks unresolved identities and any emitted report is treated as partial rather than complete.
Two or more observed worker result records represent the same launched item identity; accounting records duplicates and complete labeling is withheld unless a future duplicate policy resolves them.
Some launched items return terminal failed statuses; synthesis eligibility remains subject to the open failure policy, while accounting still records those failures in the batch summary.
Some launched items reach terminal timed_out without returning usable results; synthesis may still proceed on a best-effort basis, but unresolved or timed-out items prevent complete labeling.

Observed worker result records include identities not present in the 50-item launch manifest; accounting records unknown identities and complete labeling is withheld unless a future unknown-identity policy permits them.
If a future omission-threshold or readiness policy refuses the current level of incompleteness, the control plane withholds final synthesis completion and records the batch as not complete.

If a future omission-threshold and labeling policy allow the current incompleteness, the control plane emits the best possible report from represented identities and labels it as partial according to that policy.
